source_prompt: Design and execute a workflow plan for remediating the FigOps repository review findings.

activation.reason: The remediation spans CLI runtime, MCP security, data-contract enforcement, plotting preflight, documentation, and release metadata, with separable implementation slices and shared verification gates.

objective: Bring FigOps review findings into a staged, verifiable remediation run that prioritizes correctness and safety before documentation and maintenance polish.

surfaces.locator: README.md, AGENTS.md, Research_Central_Architecture.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, docs/architecture.md, CHANGELOG.md

assumptions.claim: Correctness gaps in MCP render paths are higher priority than documentation drift.
assumptions.verify: Confirm MCP project render and CSV render tests cover the same validation contract as CLI plot execution before starting docs-only edits.

assumptions.claim: The preferred local verification path is hub_uv.py, but this machine currently lacks uv on PATH.
assumptions.verify: Run python hub_uv.py --print-env, then either install or locate uv only after user approval; otherwise use the existing external FigOps environment directly for smoke tests.

assumptions.claim: Release metadata drift is intentional if 0.17.10 is unreleased, but it must be explicit.
assumptions.verify: Check README, CHANGELOG, docs/packaging, and git tags before deciding whether to point public docs at 0.17.9 or mark 0.17.10 as development.

phases.name: Baseline And Scope Lock
phases.entry_criteria: Review findings are available and worktree status is known.
phases.exit_criteria: A baseline receipt records runnable commands, blocked commands, dependency state, and files in scope.

phases.name: P1 Correctness Remediation
phases.exit_criteria: MCP project render data-contract parity and MCP preflight target parity are implemented with focused tests.

phases.name: P2 Safety And Runtime Remediation
phases.entry_criteria: P1 tests pass or failures are isolated to unavailable local dependencies.
phases.exit_criteria: Runtime destination symlink handling, launcher drift, smoke command health, and list-root-only behavior are resolved or converted to explicit tracked decisions.

phases.name: Docs, Examples, And Policy Alignment
phases.entry_criteria: Code behavior changes have verification receipts.
phases.exit_criteria: README/version state, canonical document hierarchy, example plotting defaults, and vector preflight policy are aligned with live code.

phases.name: Independent Verification And Fan-In
phases.exit_criteria: Verifier fails to refute the fixes, no forbidden edits occurred, and final residual risks are documented.

forbidden_actions: Do not revert unrelated user changes.
Do not publish packages or create releases.
Do not install dependencies without a human gate.
Do not weaken tests to pass the run.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: markdown summary with changed files, commands, results, and residual risks
stop_conditions: P1 code behavior is implemented and tested
a human gate blocks dependency installation
a verifier refutes a claimed fix

workers.role: MCP data-contract and preflight parity implementer
forbidden_actions: Do not edit docs except test-facing comments.
Do not change public tool names.
Do not bypass write-tool guards.

stop_conditions: full data-contract validation parity is tested
supported target formats no longer map unexpectedly to nature
tests expose an unresolved design conflict

workers.role: CLI runtime and smoke health implementer
forbidden_actions: Do not install uv automatically.
Do not modify user shell profile.
Do not remove R-dependent coverage.

stop_conditions: smoke command path is fixed or docs are corrected
list-root-only behavior is implemented or removed from CLI docs
uv missing state reports an actionable doctor path

requires_escalation_for: destructive filesystem operations outside runtime test tempdirs

forbidden_actions: Do not delete user runtime directories.
Do not relax allowed-root or symlink guards.
Do not change write tools to default enabled.

stop_conditions: pre-existing runtime symlink destination is refused or safely replaced
multipanel overwrite parity with single CSV/project render is tested
test fixture cannot be written safely on the host

forbidden_actions: Do not tag releases.
Do not claim a version is published without evidence.
Do not remove compatibility aliases from docs without code changes.

stop_conditions: README and changelog version statements are explicitly reconciled
architecture drift is either corrected or marked legacy

forbidden_actions: Do not edit files.
Do not accept producer claims without checking source.
Do not ignore failing tests as environmental without evidence.

stop_conditions: all claims are confirmed or refuted

artifact_schema.validation_command: python -c "from pathlib import Path; p=Path('baseline.receipt.md'); assert p.exists() and all(k in p.read_text() for k in ['git_status','blocked_commands','initial_test_slice'])"
python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_rendering.py tests/test_mcp_read_only.py tests/test_smoke.py tests/test_uv_runtime.py -q
python -c "from pathlib import Path; p=Path('verification.report.md'); assert p.exists() and all(k in p.read_text().lower() for k in ['confirmed','refuted','unverified','residual'])"

parallelism.fan_in_rule: Fan in after each wave only when every slice produces changed-file lists, test receipts, and explicit residual risks.
barriers: P1 correctness exit gate before P2/P3 auto-progression

verification.claim_or_output: MCP project render enforces the same data-contract semantics as CLI plotting.
verification.falsifier: Construct a project with an existing CSV that violates semantic constraints and show MCP render proceeds while CLI validation fails.
verification.evidence_required: Failing regression test before fix or equivalent source proof, passing test after fix, and command output.

verification.claim_or_output: MCP visual preflight uses the requested supported target instead of silently mapping to nature.
verification.falsifier: Render or call preflight with wiley/cell/internal target and observe nature policy metadata or incompatible pass/fail.
verification.evidence_required: Target-specific test covering at least wiley or cell plus one existing nature/science case.

verification.claim_or_output: Runtime writes cannot escape or follow pre-existing symlink destinations under job_root or _latest.
verification.falsifier: Create a temp runtime root containing a symlinked job/latest destination to an outside temp directory and show render artifacts written outside runtime root.
verification.evidence_required: Symlink-focused test that fails before fix or source-level proof, then passes after fix.

verification.claim_or_output: Documented smoke/list commands match real behavior.
verification.falsifier: Run the documented commands and show failure unrelated to missing optional R runtime.
verification.evidence_required: Command log with pass, skip, or corrected documentation.

verification.claim_or_output: Release docs clearly distinguish published version from development version.
verification.falsifier: Find conflicting current-version claims between README, pyproject, changelog, roadmap, or packaging docs.
verification.evidence_required: rg output or consistency checklist after docs edits.

risk_gates.trigger: Installing uv, pytest, R, readr, or any other dependency on the user's machine.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do not install; use existing environment if available and report blocked commands.

risk_gates.trigger: Publishing, tagging, uploading to PyPI/TestPyPI, or changing release assets.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do not publish or tag; documentation may describe the required release state only.

risk_gates.trigger: Deleting runtime directories, job roots, or symlinks outside isolated test tempdirs.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do not delete; create isolated temp fixtures and fail closed.

risk_gates.trigger: Changing public MCP tool names, CLI aliases, or compatibility aliases.
risk_gates.safe_default: Preserve aliases; deprecations require explicit design approval.

risk_gates.trigger: Network lookup to confirm PyPI/GitHub latest state.
risk_gates.safe_default: Use local metadata and mark public-state claims unverified unless user approves browsing.

stop_when: P1 correctness cannot be verified due to missing dependencies and no approved fallback exists
worktree contains conflicting user edits in owned files
a required human gate is reached

invalidation_rules: Rerun a phase when any owned source file or test file for that phase changes.
Rerun baseline when Python environment, uv availability, R availability, or git branch changes.
Rerun docs alignment when pyproject version, changelog, README, or release docs change.
skip_rules: Skip docs-release wave until P1 correctness receipts exist unless only producing a planning artifact.
Skip dependency-install attempts unless a human gate approves them.

first_slice.instruction: Create the baseline receipt for C:/dev/workspace/figops, confirm git status, identify the usable Python environment, rerun the smallest available smoke/list commands, and record blocked commands without installing dependencies.
first_slice.expected_output: baseline.receipt.md with git_status, available_python_env, blocked_commands, r_runtime_status, and initial_test_slice.
first_slice.completion_check: The receipt exists and cites exact commands and exit results; no dependencies were installed.
forbidden_actions: Do not install uv or Python packages.
Do not modify source files in the baseline slice.
Do not run destructive filesystem commands.

slices.instruction: Create the baseline receipt for C:/dev/workspace/figops, confirm git status, identify the usable Python environment, rerun the smallest available smoke/list commands, and record blocked commands without installing dependencies.
slices.completion_check: Receipt includes git status, Python/env status, uv status, R status, and at least one attempted command result.
forbidden_actions: Do not install dependencies.
Do not edit implementation files.
Do not delete runtime directories.

first_wave.entry_gate: Worktree is readable and no user edit conflict blocks read-only baseline commands.
first_wave.exit_gate: Baseline receipt exists and no forbidden actions occurred.
first_wave.fan_in: Commander reviews the receipt and chooses implementation slices for wave-1.

waves.entry_gate: wave-0-baseline exit gate passed and a Python test environment is available or a no-install fallback is documented.
waves.exit_gate: Focused MCP render/preflight tests pass or failures are classified as environmental with evidence.

waves.entry_gate: wave-1-p1-correctness receipt exists and P1 claims are not refuted.
waves.exit_gate: Safety/runtime tests pass or unresolved issues are split into explicit follow-up blockers.

waves.entry_gate: Code behavior receipts exist so docs can describe actual behavior.
waves.exit_gate: Docs consistency checklist passes and public release claims avoid unverified latest-state assertions.

waves.entry_gate: All prior wave receipts and changed-file lists are available.
waves.exit_gate: Verifier report separates confirmed, refuted, and unverified items and final commands are recorded.
